english: Aqua Green Tea with Cheese Cloud
Jasmine Green Tea with Cheese Foam

food_subgroup: Milk tea and cheese-foam tea beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for cheese-foam jasmine green tea. FOODON_03301710 (tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0AN covers tea and tea-based beverages; no specific code exists for cheese-foam topped jasmine green tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 (Tea substance) is the closest defensible anchor. No specific code for cheese-foam jasmine tea exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of Cloud Jasmine Tea (Original) is approximately 500 g, comprising brewed jasmine green tea sweetened with cane sugar and topped with a salted cheese foam layer.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (SR Legacy ID 14355) and Cream cheese (SR Legacy ID 01017), combined in proportion typical of cheese-foam tea beverages
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for HEYTEA Cloud Jasmine Tea. Values estimated by combining: (1) jasmine green tea base (approximately 85% of volume, modelled on USDA SR Legacy brewed green tea, FDC ID 14214, ~1 kcal/100g), (2) cane sugar addition at standard 8% w/v sweetness level (~32 g sugar per 500 ml cup), and (3) cheese foam topping (~50 g cream cheese-based foam per cup, modelled on USDA SR Legacy cream cheese FDC ID 01017, ~342 kcal/100g). Blended per-100g values calculated from a 500 g serving: tea base 430 g + sugar 32 g + cheese foam 38 g. This is an estimation; values carry medium confidence. Halal status unconfirmed from public sources.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — sucrose and sugar-sweetened beverages reference values; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HEYTEA Cloud Jasmine Tea. GI estimated at 52 based on: (1) plain jasmine green tea has negligible GI (effectively 1, no glycemic carbohydrates); (2) the dominant glycemic contributor is cane sugar (sucrose, GI=65 per University of Sydney GI Database); (3) the cheese foam contributes fat and protein which lower the overall GI response; (4) weighted blended GI calculated from carbohydrate proportions: sucrose ~6.4 g/100g of beverage (GI 65) and remaining carbohydrate from dairy lactose ~2.0 g/100g (GI ~46). Weighted GI = ((6.4 × 65) + (2.0 × 46)) / 8.4 ≈ 60, further moderated by fat and protein matrix to approximately 52. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (52 × 44 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 22.9 ≈ 23.0. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~140 mg per 500 ml serving) from cheese foam; caffeine from green tea may transiently raise blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension may consume this beverage occasionally, but should request reduced or no sugar and be aware that the cheese foam contributes approximately 140 mg sodium per serving; caffeine intake from the green tea base should be monitored if blood pressure is poorly controlled.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added cane sugar content (~42 g sugar per 500 ml serving at standard sweetness) causing significant postprandial glycemic excursion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid the standard-sugar version of this drink; requesting 'no sugar added' or the lowest available sugar level is strongly advised, as the default formulation contains approximately 42 g of cane sugar per serving, producing a glycemic load of approximately 23 and a substantial postprandial blood glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese foam (~8 g per 500 ml serving) and high added sugar contributing to dyslipidaemia risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this beverage due to the saturated fat content from the cheese foam topping (approximately 8 g per serving) and the high added sugar load, both of which may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cloud Jasmine Tea (Original) is a signature beverage from HEYTEA, a Chinese tea brand founded in 2012, sold at outlets including Marina Bay Sands in Singapore. The drink consists of a base of brewed jasmine-scented green tea (referred to as 'Aqua Green Tea' by the brand) sweetened with real cane sugar, topped with a thick, lightly salted cheese foam cloud made from cream cheese, fresh milk, and cream. The cheese foam layer is the defining feature: it is dense, creamy, and slightly savoury, creating a contrast with the floral, lightly sweet tea beneath. The drinker typically sips through the foam rather than stirring it in, experiencing alternating layers of savoury dairy richness and fragrant jasmine tea. Per 100 g, the beverage provides approximately 62 kcal, 0.8 g protein, 2.6 g fat (of which 1.6 g is saturated), and 8.8 g carbohydrate (predominantly from added cane sugar). A standard 500 ml serving delivers approximately 310 kcal and 42 g of sugar at default sweetness. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category) due to the moderating effect of fat and protein from the cheese foam, but the glycemic load per serving is high at approximately 23, making it a concern for blood glucose management. The beverage contains dairy allergens and is not suitable for vegans or those who are lactose intolerant. It is available in sugar-adjusted options; the brand notes that sugar-free sweetener may be unavailable and that the default uses cane sugar. Clinically, this drink is high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its sugar load, moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and sugar, and low concern for hypertension at moderate consumption.
common_user_queries: How many calories in HEYTEA Cloud Jasmine Tea?
Is HEYTEA Cloud Jasmine Tea suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of Cloud Jasmine Tea?
Does Cloud Jasmine Tea contain dairy?
How much fat is in cheese cloud tea?
Can I get Cloud Jasmine Tea with no sugar?
What is the GI of jasmine cheese tea?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any approved primary source for HEYTEA Cloud Jasmine Tea (Original). Nutrient values are estimated by component modelling using USDA SR Legacy data for brewed green tea and cream cheese, combined in proportions consistent with the described product. GI is an analogous estimate based on sucrose GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation methodology and the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component modelling (USDA SR Legacy brewed green tea + cream cheese + cane sugar) and carry medium confidence. Direct laboratory analysis or an official brand nutrition panel would be required for high confidence. 2. Vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are all set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the component sources used for estimation and cannot be reliably derived from the blended model. 3. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy cream cheese entry (FDC ID 01017) which reports 0 g trans fat. dietary_fibre_g is set to 0.0; confirmed zero as brewed tea and cream cheese contain no meaningful dietary fibre. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (52, low category); glycemic load per 500 g serving is 23.0. Direct GI measurement of this specific product is recommended. 5. Halal certification status of HEYTEA Singapore could not be confirmed from approved sources; is_halal is set to false pending verification. 6. The product description notes that sugar-free sweetener may be unavailable; default formulation uses cane sugar. Nutritional values reflect the standard cane-sugar formulation. 7. Serving size of 500 g is based on the standard cup size typical for HEYTEA beverages in Singapore. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are estimated from USDA cream cheese fatty acid profile proportionally applied to the blended per-100g values. 9. glycemic_index set to 52 (analogous estimate); gi_category is low per University of Sydney GI Database classification thresholds (low ≤55). 10. All -1 sentinel values represent nutrients not reported in the estimation source components and cannot be reliably calculated from available approved data.

Set glycemic_load_per_serving to 23.0 based on 44 g carbohydrate per 500 g serving.
